1.solution:
Buffalo in 8d = Goat in 18d → B=9G/4. Cow in 15d = Buffalo in 12d → 15C=12B → C=9G/5.
Goat+Cow: (G+9G/5)×101 = W → 14G×101/5 = W.
All three: G+9G/4+9G/5 = 101G/20/day. Time = (1414G/5)/(101G/20) = 5656/101 = 56.
Answer: 56.

2.solution:
Total fill rate = 1/12+1/18+1/20+1/24+1/40 = 92/360 = 23/90. Net rate for 3h fill = 1/180.
23/90 − 2(r_i+r_j) = 1/180 → r_i+r_j = 1/8.
Only pair: 1/12+1/24=1/8. So A and D are emptying. B, C, E are filling.
→ D is NOT a filling pipe.
Answer: C (D).

3.solution:
P fills 360L in 24min → 15 L/min. Q drains in 36min → 10 L/min. 87.5%=315L, 12.5%=45L.
Cycle 1: P(0→315L)=21min, Q(315→45L)=27min. End: t=48, tank=45L.
Subsequent cycles: P(45→315)=18min, Q(315→45)=27min. Each=45min.
t=48, 93, 138: tank=45L each time. t=138: P opens. At t=156: tank=315L, Q opens.
t=180: Q open 24min, drains 240L → 315−240=75L.
Answer: 75.

5.solution:
Total work = 18N man-days. In 24 days with 10 dropping each day:
Σ(k=1 to 24)[N−10(k−1)] = 24N − 10×(0+1+…+23) = 24N − 2760 = 18N.
6N = 2760 → N = 460.
Answer: 460.

6.solution:
During minute n, taps 1 to n open → n(n+1)/2 L added.
Total in N minutes = Σn(n+1)/2 = N(N+1)(N+2)/6.
N(N+1)(N+2)/6 = 2024 → N(N+1)(N+2) = 12144.
N=22: 22×23×24 = 12144. ✓
Answer: 22.

8.solution:
A: 37.5% in 36min → full in 96min → rate=1/96.
B: 43.75% in 42min → full in 96min → rate=1/96.
C rate = 1/30−1/96−1/96 = 1/30−1/48 = 3/240 = 1/80.
C fills 62.5%=5/8 of tank: time=(5/8)×80=50 min.
Answer: 50.

9.solution:
W∈[2M/3, M], C∈[M/3, 2M/3]. Min days of 9M+6W+9C: maximize rates → W=M, C=2M/3.
Max rate=9M+6M+6M=21M. T=144×21M=3024M.
Max days of 8M+9W+12C: minimize rates → W=2M/3, C=M/3.
Min rate=8M+6M+4M=18M. Days=3024M/18M=168.
Answer: A (168).

10.solution:
f/9−d/18=1/3 with f+d=k → 2f−d=6 → 3f=k+6 → f=(k+6)/3.
Integer solution: k≡0(mod 3). Drain pipes d=(2k−6)/3≥1 → k≥5 → k≥6 (multiples of 3).
k∈{6,9,12,...,48}: (48−6)/3+1=15 values.
Answer: A (15).
